title: Write a minimal valid C++ program
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write the smallest valid C++ program that compiles and runs successfully.

## Input

None — this program takes no input.

## Output

None — this program produces no visible output. The exit code is what matters.

Do **not** use `#include`, `cout`, `cin`, or any library features — they have not been taught yet.

title: Use statements to compute and print a value
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a program that uses declaration statements and expression statements to perform a simple computation, then prints the result.

## Input

None — this program takes no input.

## Output

Print a single integer on its own line: the result of adding `3` and `4` (should be `7`).

*Note:* The `std::cout` line is provided for you — just make sure `result` holds the right value before it runs.
